revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:稲荷前古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Inarimae Kofun Group | alternate_name = | image = [[File:Inarimae Kofungun.jpg|300px]] | caption = Kofun No. 16 (center-left) and No. 15 (right) | location ={{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1203361|lt=Aoba Ward, Yokohama|1=Aoba-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Ōba Town, Yokohama|ja|大場町 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba Town, Yokohama|1=Ōbachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433901}} 156-10 (Ōba Town, Inarimae<ref name="Prefectural Record">{{PDF|[http://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/uploaded/attachment/847853.pdf Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site Inventory]}} - Kanagawa Prefecture (Accessed May 16, 2017, at 10:31 PM {{ill|Japan Standard Time|en|Japan Standard Time|qid=Q909085|lt=JST|1=Japan Standard Time}})</ref>); {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}| part_of = | location coordinates = | coordinates = 35| coordinates = 33| coordinates = 34.66 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 31| coordinates =54.94; {{Coord|35.559628|N|139.531928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-14 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tunnel grave|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tunnel grave|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} (total of 19 burial sites) | built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} - {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | map_size = Kofun No. 16: Total length of 37.5 [[:en:Meters|Meters]] | Interred_individual = Successive chieftains and their families from the Totsuka region | burial goods = Beads, iron weapons, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | alternate_name = Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Historic Site = | notes = | Archaeological site package number = | map = Japan Kanagawa | type = Kofun Group | map_dot_label = Inarimae Kofun Group | map_dot_label = bottom | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Inarimae Kofun Group''' (稲荷前古墳群) is a Kofun group located in {{ill|Ōba Town, Aoba Ward|ja|大場町 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba Town, Aoba Ward|1=Ōbachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433901}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. This group features a variety of kofun (ancient tumuli), including {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, {{ill|round tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|square tumuli|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|tunnel graves|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tunnel graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}, all densely concentrated in a small area. Once referred to as a "museum of tumuli"{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|p=341}}, today only three tumuli remain. The group is Historic Site1 as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|33|39.3|N|139|31|52.4|E}} | zoom = 16 | float = right | width = 265| height =300 | caption = Ancient tomb locations reconstructed from {{ill|Midori Ward History|ja|緑区史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori Ward History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|横浜市緑区|1985|p=62}}. Red map_dot_labels indicate destroyed tombs, blue map_dot_labels indicate extant tombs. | map_sizemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|33|37.9|N|139|31|54.2|E}} | map_dot_label1= No. 1 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos1=left | mark-title1=No. 1 Kofun | map_dot_label-size1=12 | map_dot_label-color1=red | mark-size1=28 | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|33|38.6|N|139|31|53.5|E}} | map_dot_label2= No. 2 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos2= left | mark-title2=No. 2 Kofun | map_dot_label-size2=12 | map_dot_label-color2=red | mark-size2=15 | mark2=円墳.png
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|33|40.1|N|139|31|50.9|E}} | map_dot_label3= No. 3 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos3=right | mark-title3=No. 3 Kofun | map_dot_label-size3=12 | map_dot_label-color3=red | mark-size3=25 | mark3=円墳.png
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|33|41.1|N|139|31|50.5|E}} | map_dot_label4= No. 5 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos4=right | mark-title4=No. 5 Kofun | map_dot_label-size4=12 | map_dot_label-color4=red | mark-size4=18 | mark4=円墳.png
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|33|46.7|N|139|31|50.1|E}} | map_dot_label5= No. 6 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos5=top | mark-title5=No. 6 Kofun | map_dot_label-size5=12 | map_dot_label-color5=red | mark-size5=20 | mark5=前方後円墳-南西.png
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|33|37.3|N|139|31|55.3|E}} | map_dot_label6= No. 13 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos6=right | mark-title6=No. 13 Kofun | map_dot_label-size6=12 | map_dot_label-color6=red | mark-size6=18 | mark6=円墳.png
| mark-coord7= {{Coord|35|33|36.3|N|139|31|54.4|E}} | map_dot_label7= No. 14 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos7=right | mark-title7=No. 14 Kofun | map_dot_label-size7=12 | map_dot_label-color7=red | mark-size7=18 | mark7=円墳.png
| mark-coord8= {{Coord|35|33|35.5|N|139|31|54.2|E}} | map_dot_label8= No. 15 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos8=right | mark-title8=No. 15 Kofun | map_dot_label-size8=12 | map_dot_label-color8=blue | mark-size8=20 | mark8=方墳.png
| mark-coord9= {{Coord|35|33|34.7|N|139|31|54.2|E}} | map_dot_label9= No. 16 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos9=right | mark-title9=No. 16 Kofun | map_dot_label-size9=12 | map_dot_label-color9=blue | mark-size9=25 | mark9=前方後方墳-北.png
| mark-coord10= {{Coord|35|33|33.7|N|139|31|54.9|E}} | map_dot_label10= No. 17 Kofun | map_dot_label-pos10=right | mark-title10=No. 17 Kofun | map_dot_label-size10=12 | map_dot_label-color10=blue | mark-size10=20 | mark10=方墳.png
| mark-coord11= {{Coord|35|33|45.4|N|139|31|55.0|E}} | map_dot_label11= Horizontal Tomb Cluster A | map_dot_label-pos11=right | mark-title11=Horizontal Tomb Cluster A | map_dot_label-size11=12 | map_dot_label-color11=red | mark-size11=20 | mark11=横穴墓.png
| mark-coord12= {{Coord|35|33|46.5|N|139|31|51.1|E}} | map_dot_label12= Horizontal Tomb Cluster B | map_dot_label-pos12=right | mark-title12=Horizontal Tomb Cluster B | map_dot_label-size12=12 | map_dot_label-color12=red | mark-size12=20 | mark12=横穴墓.png
| mark-coord13= {{Coord|35|33|37.0|N|139|31|53.5|E}} | map_dot_label13= Horizontal Tomb Cluster C | map_dot_label-pos13=Left | mark-title13=Horizontal Tomb Cluster C | map_dot_label-size13=12 | map_dot_label-color13=red | mark-size13=20 | mark13=横穴墓.png }} [[File:Inarimae Kofun Group in residential area.png|thumb|300px|Inarimae Kofun Group surrounded by developed residential areas.]] The nearest {{ill|railway station|en|鉄道駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/railway station|qq=}} is {{ill|Ichigao Station|en|Ichigao Station|qid=Q11479967|lt=Ichigao Station|1=Ichigao Station}} on the {{ill|Den-en-toshi Line|en|Den-en-toshi Line|qid=Q1190086|lt=Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line|1=Den-en-toshi Line}}. The tumulus is located on a hill facing the {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture Route 12|ja|神奈川県道12号横浜上麻生線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture Route 12|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Road Route 12|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589243}}.
During the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (4th to 7th centuries), a powerful local ruler governed {{ill|Totsuka District|ja|都筑郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka District|1=Tsuzuki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11643787}}, which extended along the Tsurumi River watershed. This area was connected to the Yamato Court. In addition to the Inarimae Kofun Group, other tumuli and horizontal tombs, such as the {{ill|Chokōji Kofun Group|ja|朝光寺原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokōji Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ichigao Horizontal Tomb Group|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群|1=Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Horizontal Tomb Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}}, have been discovered in {{ill|Ichigao Town|ja|市ケ尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Town|1=Ichigaochō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479968}}. These tombs are believed to have served as burial sites for successive local rulers and their families.
In 1965, during Japan’s rapid economic growth, the {{ill|Kōhoku New Town|ja|港北ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhoku New Town|1=Kōhoku New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563029}} plan, one of Yokohama’s six major development projects, was announced. By 1966, the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line had opened, and the northern Yokohama hills were being developed into residential areas{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|p=269}}. Although Inarimae Kofun Group’s site in Ōba Town, Midori Ward (now Aoba Ward), was not part of Kōhoku New Town, the opening of the Den-en-toshi Line spurred residential development in the surrounding area{{Sfn|Yokohama History Museum|1998|pp=25-31}}. The Inarimae Kofun Group was discovered during land preparation for residential housing on June 15, 1967{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|p=300}}.
Archaeologists, led by Ken Amakasu and Isamu Okamoto, conducted excavations in August 1967 and from February 20 to March 31, 1969, to protect the tumuli from imminent development. The investigations uncovered ten tumuli, including two {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Nos. 1 and 6), one {{ill|rectangular tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (No. 16), four {{ill|round tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and three {{ill|square tumuli|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, along with nine horizontal tombs, earning the site the nickname “Museum of Ancient Tumuli”{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|pp=300-342}}.
Although preservation efforts arose due to the importance of keyhole and rectangular tumuli, which were rare upstream of the Tsurumi River, then-Mayor {{ill|Ichio Asukata|en|Ichio Asukata|qid=Q5986270|lt=Asukata Kazuo|1=Ichio Asukata}} made dismissive remarks during a site visit, such as, "You can't save every sardine's head,"{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|p=346}} indicating a lack of interest in preservation. Furthermore, No. 1 Kofun became physically unsalvageable when development work shaved off the surrounding earth, creating steep cliffs. Former Shimane University professor Yoshinori Tanaka, who participated in the excavation, later stated in his book that this was a deliberate move by developers to force abandonment of preservation efforts{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|p=313}}. Ultimately, most of the site was destroyed and converted into residential areas.
Today, three tumuli—No. 16 (rectangular tumulus), No. 15 (square tumulus), and No. 17 (square tumulus)—are preserved. On March 24, 1970, the site was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Historic Site|ja|神奈川県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{R|Prefecture Register}}<ref name="City Register">{{PDF|[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/pdf/shiiki-bunkazai2016dd04.pdf List of National, Prefectural, and Municipal Historic Site]}} - {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=City of Yokohama|1=Yokohama}} (Accessed May 16, 2017, JST).</ref>. The preserved area covers 8,056.63 square meters, owned and managed by the City of Yokohama{{R|City Register}}.
==Photographs==
<gallery> File:Inarimae Kofun Group-15, funkyu.jpg|Mound No. 15 image:Number 16 Kofun in Inarimae Kofun Group.jpg|Mound No. 16 File:Inarimae Kofun Group-17, funkyu.jpg|Mound No. 17 </gallery>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Mound No. 15 (with Mound No. 16 in the foreground) |- | {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-15&16, Funkyu 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|Mound}} || {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-15, Top 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|Summit}} |}
{| class="wikitable" |+ Mound No. 16 - Mound |- | {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-16, Funkyu West 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|West Side}} || {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-16, Funkyu East 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|East Side}} |}
{| class="wikitable" |+ Mound No. 16 - Summit |- | {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-16, Zenpo-bu Top 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|Front Section}} || {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-16, Kubire-bu Top 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|Neck Section}} || {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-16, Koen-bu Top 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|Rear Section}} |}
{| class="wikitable" |+ Mound No. 17 - Mound |- | {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-17, Funkyu North 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|North Side}} || {{panoViewer2|Inarimae Kofun Group-17, Funkyu East 360-degree.jpg|center|240px|East Side}} |}
==References==
{{Citation help}} {{reflist}}
==Bibliography== ===Cited Works=== *{{Cite book|author=City of Yokohama Midori Ward|title=History of Midori Ward, Yokohama: Volume 1|chapter=Inarimae Kofun Group|publisher=City of Yokohama|year=1985|date=1985-03-31|pages=61-70|ncid=BN09184065|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|last=Tanaka|first=Yoshiaki|title=Development and Archaeology: The Era of Ichigao Yokoanabos, Sanden Plateau Ruins, and Inarimae Kofun Group|chapter=Chapter 4: Destroyed Sites and Transtypeed Regions|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2019|date=2019-07-15|pages=285-404|ncid=BB28625447|isbn=9784787719096|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Yokohama City History Museum|authorlink=Yokohama City History Museum|chapter=4. Excavations of the 1960s|title=Yokohama Excavation Stories: A Visual History of Excavation|publisher=Yokohama City Cultural Foundation|pages=25-31|year=1998|date=1998-03-07|ncid=BA37874376|ref=harv}}
===Related Works (Non-Cited)=== *{{Cite book|author=Survey Group of Yokohama Northern Archaeological Heritage|title=Report on Archaeological Heritage in Northern Yokohama: Interim Report|chapter=Excavation of Inarimae Kofun Group: Summary Report|publisher=Survey Group of Yokohama Northern Archaeological Heritage|year=1968|date=1968-03-31|pages=34-55|ncid=BA91073088|ref=harv}}
==External Links== {{Commonscat|Inarimae Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/aoba/shokai/bunkazai/a/bunkazai001.html Inarimae Kofun Group - City of Yokohama, Aoba Ward]
